alternative_architectures.name: Perfect-memory individual learner
alternative_architectures.verdict: Can accumulate knowledge without social criticism if internal error update is unbiased.

alternative_architectures.name: Collective hive mind
alternative_architectures.verdict: Social mechanisms collapse into internal cognitive functions; criticism is replaceable by internal model comparison.

alternative_architectures.name: Non-symbolic gradient learner
alternative_architectures.verdict: Violates human-readable forms but preserves primitive functions through weights and gradients.

alternative_architectures.name: Evolution-only civilization
alternative_architectures.verdict: Cumulative adaptation occurs slowly without explicit science; it satisfies weaker knowledge accumulation but not explanatory science.

alternative_architectures.name: Perfect quantum communication collective
alternative_architectures.verdict: Communication changes implementations, not primitive requirements.

alternative_architectures.name: Brute-force oracle search
alternative_architectures.verdict: If exhaustive search and truth access are free, compression and refinement become convenient but unnecessary.

derivations.derivation: From A1, some state must persist. A literal research database is replaceable, but retention is necessary.
derivations.lost_if_removed: Earlier evidence cannot constrain later belief; cumulative knowledge collapses into repeated first attempts.

derivations.derivation: Criticism is one social implementation of error-sensitive update over discriminable alternatives.
derivations.lost_if_removed: Errors can persist without a channel that forces alternatives to compete against evidence.

derivations.derivation: Failures must affect future updates, but they need not be preserved as human-readable cases.
derivations.lost_if_removed: The system repeats known bad regions or overgeneralizes beyond defeated scope.

derivations.derivation: When alternatives are represented badly, improvement requires changing the question or representation.
derivations.lost_if_removed: The system remains trapped in a representation that may make improvement impossible or inefficient.

derivations.derivation: Compression follows from resource-bounded reuse, not from cumulative knowledge alone.
derivations.lost_if_removed: Without reusable structure, the system may still learn by lookup if resources are abundant, but loses efficiency under scarcity.

derivations.derivation: Any reliable cumulative learner must encode, infer, or test where learned structure applies.
derivations.lost_if_removed: The system transfers claims into contexts where they do not hold, corrupting cumulative knowledge.

primitives.derivation: If performance must improve over time, information from earlier interactions must affect later interactions.
primitives.name: Temporal retention

primitives.derivation: If predictions can be wrong, the system needs a way for mismatch to change future behavior.
primitives.name: Error-sensitive update

primitives.derivation: Improvement requires distinguishing at least two possible policies, models, or explanations.
primitives.name: Discriminable alternatives

primitives.derivation: A learned claim can improve future performance only where its conditions of transfer are not completely lost.
primitives.name: Scope tracking

primitives.derivation: When learning or computation is costly, successful structure must be reused rather than rediscovered from scratch.
primitives.name: Resource-bounded reuse

primitives.derivation: If the current question or representation causes avoidable error, cumulative improvement needs a way to change it.
primitives.name: Representation revision
